title: brain-plugin.toml
description: Brain plugin manifest — declares a plugin's identity, MCP server command, sensitivity tier, and surfaces.

id.description: Unique plugin identifier. Used as the MCP tool namespace prefix (e.g. 'halvor' → halvor.*).

tier.description: Sensitivity tier. Enforced at federation and search routing layers.

description.description: Human-readable description shown in brain plugin list.

context_injection.description: How much context this plugin injects into agent system prompts when Brain MCP is reachable. 'minimal' = tool list only; 'full' = topology + live state; 'none' = no injection.

command.description: Command to start the plugin's MCP server (stdio transport). Brain registers this as an MCP server and federates its tools under the plugin.id namespace.

args.description: Arguments passed to the MCP server command.

env.description: Environment variables injected when starting the MCP server.

publishes.description: Common interface types this plugin federates. Brain validates federation publishes against the declared interfaces.

max_command_level.description: Maximum permission level any command in this plugin can request. Brain rejects commands that exceed this at registration time.

read_federation.description: Sensitivity tiers this plugin can read from the federation index.

write_federation.description: Federation channels this plugin can write to (other plugins' channels). Empty means own channel only.

sensitive_access.description: Whether this plugin can read or write sensitive values (credentials, keys).

manifest_dir.description: Path (relative to this manifest file) containing agent .md files. Brain loads these agents when the plugin is active, namespacing them under the plugin id.

path.description: Path (relative to this manifest file) to the docs directory. Brain indexes these into the unified search under the plugin's source label.

contributes_to.description: Brain UI surfaces this plugin contributes to.

enabled.description: Whether this Brain node participates in DB sync.

peers.description: Peer Brain nodes to sync with (e.g. 'brain@hotel.local').
